added.title: `AGENTS.md` â€” `File role map` appendix (`Â§File role map`)
added.body: classifying every top-level entry file by architectural role: **SoT** (canonical â€” `AGENTS.md` itself, `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md`, other `docs/*.md`, `skills/**`, `schemas/**` + `templates/**`), **thin-bridge** (`CLAUDE.md`, `GEMINI.md`, `.windsurfrules`, `.cursor/rules/engineering-workflow.mdc`), **runtime role-spec** (`.cursor/rules/{planner,implementer,reviewer}.mdc`, `agents/{planner,implementer,reviewer}.md`), and **reference wrapper** (`reference-implementations/roles/*.md`, `reference-implementations/validator-*/`). Ends with a "Where a new rule belongs" decision guide and the invariant that closes the map: *a normative claim appears in exactly one SoT file; every consumer cites that section by name*. The map is the index the repo was missing â€” without it, `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` had drifted into treating `agents/implementer.md Â§Pre-handoff self-check` and `agents/reviewer.md Â§Anti-rationalization rules` as normative sources of truth, which inverted the intended SoT â†” bridge relationship.

added.title: `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` Â§Pre-handoff self-check
added.body: new canonical subsection inside `Â§Three canonical roles â†’ Implementer` containing the five Implementer questions (acceptance-criterion coverage, verification coverage, reference existence, pattern alignment, evidence-path completion), the "vague / hedged answer is a failing answer" rule, the `implementation_notes` recording protocol, and the Lean / Zero-ceremony mode application. Body text moved verbatim from `agents/implementer.md`; the normative rule now lives with the role that must clear it.

added.title: `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` Â§Anti-rationalization rules
added.body: new canonical subsection inside `Â§Three canonical roles â†’ Reviewer` containing the six Reviewer send-back triggers (perfect-confidence hallucination, hedging language, unsubstantiated `pass` entries, read-only review, editing through the back door, thin residual-risk section), the "heuristic failure mirrors" closing note, and the "any one trigger applying is a mandatory send-back, not a judgment call" enforcement clause. Body text moved verbatim from `agents/reviewer.md`.

added.title: `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` TL;DR canonical-contract declaration
added.body: explicitly stating that runtime bridges (`agents/*.md`, `.cursor/rules/*.mdc`, `reference-implementations/roles/*.md`) translate the contract into runtime-specific agent configuration but do not introduce new normative rules â€” if a bridge states a rule this document does not, the bridge yields. Pointer to `AGENTS.md Â§File role map` for the full ownership table. Previously the file was de facto canonical but did not self-declare; bridges were free to ship normative content that the contract did not, which is exactly the architecture inversion this release fixes.

added.title: `.github/scripts/check-summary-drift.py`
added.body: pre-release lint that auto-discovers `<number> <noun>` TL;DR claims, counts matching structural entries in the body, and flags mismatches for the noun vocabulary the original 1.14.4 audit caught (axes, anti-patterns, patterns, steps, stages, rules, principles, fracture lines, coordination strategies, hook points). Replaces the one-off `docs/audits/summary-vs-per-case-registry.md` audit artifact (214 lines) with a continuously-enforced CI lint. Wired into `.github/workflows/validate.yml` as the `summary-drift` job. Running against current repo state: 37 docs scanned, no drift.

added.title: `CHANGELOG.archive.md` / `CHANGELOG.archive.json` / `ROADMAP.archive.md`
added.body: pre-1.14.0 releases (1.0.0â€“1.13.1) and their matching closed initiatives moved to archive files preserving every entry verbatim per `CLAUDE.md Â§3`. Active files retain Unreleased + 1.14.0 onward and point at the archive. `CHANGELOG.md` shrinks from 465 â†’ 170 lines; `ROADMAP.md` shrinks from 715 â†’ 241 lines. `CHANGELOG.json` regenerated to match the shortened active `CHANGELOG.md`; the full pre-archive snapshot is kept in `CHANGELOG.archive.json`.

changed.title: `agents/implementer.md Â§Pre-handoff self-check`
changed.body: reduced to a three-sentence pointer to `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md Â§Pre-handoff self-check` instead of restating the five questions. The body (intro + five questions + recording protocol + mode application) no longer lives here â€” it lives in the canonical handoff document. The Claude Code sub-agent frontmatter (`tools:` row, `model:`, `description:`) is unchanged; this file remains the runtime bridge that translates the matrix row into a Claude Code sub-agent definition.

changed.title: `agents/reviewer.md Â§Anti-rationalization rules`
changed.body: reduced to a two-sentence pointer to `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md Â§Anti-rationalization rules`. The six triggers, the "heuristic failure mirrors" clause, and the enforcement wording all live at the canonical source now; this file no longer restates them.

changed.title: `reference-implementations/roles/implementer.md Â§Pre-handoff self-check`
changed.body: and **`reference-implementations/roles/reviewer.md Â§Anti-rationalization rules`** reduced to pointers for the same reason. These paste-ready role prompts were previously holding a second unofficial SoT of the two blocks (verbatim-identical to `agents/*.md` lines 34â€“46 / 30â€“41 in their respective files). The new pointer keeps the paste-ready property (the reader still sees the section heading and a one-paragraph "what this is" line) while eliminating the duplicate-SoT drift risk.

changed.title: Consumers of the two moved blocks repointed to canonical SoT
changed.body: per `CLAUDE.md Â§5` cross-cutting-term discipline: `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md` (Rule 5a auditable-on-request clause + the anti-metric paragraph on Phase 4 re-entry frequency), `docs/change-manifest-spec.md Â§parallel_groups` (Reviewer-audits-this-field paragraph), `docs/ai-operating-contract.md Â§2a Reviewer's sampling right`, `docs/operational-cheat-sheet.md` (Implementer top-5 item 5), `skills/engineering-workflow/phases/phase4-implement.md` (Pre-handoff self-check section + See-also list), `skills/engineering-workflow/phases/fix-retest-loop.md` (re-entry close-the-loop sentence), `skills/engineering-workflow/references/parallelization-patterns.md` (two rules references in Pattern B + Relationship section), `skills/engineering-workflow/references/cluster-parallelism.md` (per-cluster Implementer self-check bullet + What-this-document-is-not clause), `reference-implementations/roles/role-composition-patterns.md` (Relationship section). Pointers to `agents/*.md` for content that did not move (Read-manifest-in-full, evidence audit, sampling right, send-back authority) are intentionally left unchanged.

changed.title: `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` reading-order guide (Pair 1, Pair 3)
changed.body: updated to reference local `Â§Pre-handoff self-check` / `Â§Anti-rationalization rules` instead of `agents/implementer.md` / `agents/reviewer.md`. The machine-readable pre-filter section's two mentions of `agents/*.md` at `Â§Pre-filter checks` and `Â§Hard limits` are likewise updated.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md` absorbs the phase Ă— mode matrix
changed.body: . The eight files at `skills/engineering-workflow/references/phase-minimums/phase{0..7}-minimums.md` (11â€“15 lines each, re-classifying the same axes) are replaced by a single `## Phase minimums` section in `SKILL.md` â€” eight rows Ă— two columns (Lean / Full). `skills/engineering-workflow/README.md` and `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md Â§How this composes` redirect to the new section.

changed.title: Three mode-selection decision aids repointed to `skills/engineering-workflow/references/mode-decision-tree.md`
changed.body: (the existing execution-layer SoT): `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md Â§Decision table` (9-row summary table), `skills/engineering-workflow/references/startup-checklist.md Â§5 Mode selection` (four-bullet mode-criterion summary), and `docs/onboarding/when-not-to-use-this.md Â§60-second decision flow` (full Q1â€“Q4 decision tree) now each are a 1-line pointer at `mode-decision-tree.md`. The forced-Full list divergence resolved in 1.15.0 was the specific failure mode this removes: three parallel copies of the same list inevitably drift.

removed.title: `docs/audits/summary-vs-per-case-registry.md`
removed.body: (214 lines) â€” superseded by `.github/scripts/check-summary-drift.py`. The audit was a one-off meta-record produced 2026-04-22; all eight (a) real-drift findings were resolved in 1.14.4 + the one cross-reference finding in 1.14.5. Historical CHANGELOG / ROADMAP references to the file (in the archived 1.14.4 / 1.14.5 entries) are preserved verbatim per `CLAUDE.md Â§3`.

removed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/phase-minimums/phase{0..7}-minimums.md`
removed.body: (eight files) â€” inlined into `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md Â§Phase minimums`.

changed.title: Four execution modes are now first-class citizens of the glossary.
changed.body: Prior to this release, `docs/principles.md Â§8` named four modes ("no-process path, three-line delivery, Lean, Full") but the execution layer (`SKILL.md`, `mode-decision-tree.md`, `startup-checklist.md`, `glossary.md`) only defined Lean and Full. The missing two were referenced in other files under five different names (`no-process path`, `ultra-lean`, `No skill`, `three-line handoff`, `stripped-down version` for the lowest mode; `three-line handoff` vs `three-line delivery` for the gray-zone mode), with no single canonical home. `docs/glossary.md Â§Execution mode` now defines all four â€” **Zero-ceremony, Three-line delivery, Lean, Full** â€” as the normative names, with per-mode artifact minimums, selection criteria, and an explicit "legacy aliases (retired)" note mapping the five old names to their canonical equivalents. The glossary is the SoT; other files cite it rather than redefining.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/mode-decision-tree.md` rewritten
changed.body: to be the execution-layer source of truth for mode selection. It now has five scenario tables â€” forced-Full, forced-Lean, forced-Three-line-delivery, forced-Zero-ceremony, and mode upgrade/downgrade triggers â€” covering all four modes. Prior versions only distinguished forced-Full vs forced-Lean. Forced-Full triggers are consolidated into a single canonical list (DB migration / public API breaking / enum consumer-visible / payments / auth / PII / cross-team handoff / long-lived feature-flag / staged rollout); earlier the list was split across `mode-decision-tree.md` and `docs/onboarding/when-not-to-use-this.md Â§Q3` with mismatches (`enum` + `rollout` appeared only in the onboarding doc; `cross-team` + `long-lived flag` + `PII` appeared only in the decision tree). Other decision aids (`SKILL.md Â§Decision table`, `startup-checklist.md Â§5`, `when-not-to-use-this.md Â§60-second decision flow`) now cite this file rather than carry parallel lists.

changed.title: `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md` gains a master ceremony-scaling table
changed.body: at the top of the six-rules section, spelling out how each of Rules 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5 / 5a / 6 applies in each of the four execution modes. Previously only Rule 5a had explicit ceremony scaling (`Zero-ceremony waived / Lean once / Full every boundary`); the other rules were written as if they applied uniformly, implying a Lean bugfix still needed six named gates plus six commits plus a ROADMAP row per step. The master table makes the real scaling explicit: **Lean mode compresses all six rules into a single gate event at Lean-5**, **Three-line delivery waives phase-gate discipline entirely** (the commit message is the record), and **Zero-ceremony is fully waived**. Rule 5a's existing scaling block is preserved and extended to include the Three-line-delivery row. The "single P0 + P7 pair" sentence for single-change initiatives is scoped to Full mode, resolving a contradiction with `AGENTS.md Â§6`'s previous "Trivial single-phase tasks are exempt from the ROADMAP rule" wording.

changed.title: `AGENTS.md Â§6`
changed.body: updated to state the five rules' Full-mode-only application scope up-front, then point at the phase-gate master ceremony-scaling table. Evidence-before-completion remains universal (every mode). The previous exemption sentence ("Trivial single-phase tasks are exempt") is replaced with the four-mode scaling clause that references the canonical ceremony-scaling table.

changed.title: `docs/principles.md Â§8`
changed.body: updated to use the canonical mode names (Zero-ceremony / Three-line delivery / Lean / Full) and point at `glossary.md Â§Execution mode` and `mode-decision-tree.md` as the canonical sources rather than standing alone.

changed.title: `docs/onboarding/when-not-to-use-this.md`
changed.body: updated to use canonical mode names (Zero-ceremony instead of "No-process path"; Three-line delivery instead of "stripped-down version" / "three-line handoff"). The 60-second decision flow now covers all four modes explicitly (Q1 â†’ Zero-ceremony, Q2 â†’ Zero-ceremony, Q3 â†’ forced-Full check, Q4 â†’ Three-line delivery vs Lean). The edge-case quick-reference table uses canonical names. The forced-Full trigger list is delegated to `mode-decision-tree.md` rather than duplicated.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md`
changed.body: updated: the frontmatter description lists four modes instead of two. The `Â§Mode selection` block has four sub-sections (Zero-ceremony / Three-line delivery / Lean / Full) with per-mode artifact minimums. The `Â§Decision table` adds rows for Zero-ceremony and Three-line delivery; the ghost term "ultra-lean" (referenced but never defined anywhere in the repo) is removed. The `Â§Lean workflow` section now explicitly notes Lean-0 â€¦ Lean-5 are **steps, not phases** â€” phase-gate discipline applies once at Lean-5, not per step â€” and points at the `glossary.md Â§Lean â†’ Full step / phase correspondence` mapping table for upgrade cases. The `Â§Artifact guidance` section adds Zero-ceremony and Three-line-delivery rows.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/startup-checklist.md Â§5 and Â§6`
changed.body: rewritten to cover all four modes. Â§5 now explicitly lists each mode's selection criterion; Â§6 lists each mode's artifact minimum. Both point at `mode-decision-tree.md` as the selection SoT and `phase-gate-discipline.md Â§Ceremony scaling` for rule applicability.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/misuse-signals.md`
changed.body: Â§Misuse 1 and Â§Misuse 2 rewritten to cover downgrading to / upgrading from any of the four modes, not just Leanâ†”Full. Each misuse block now references `mode-decision-tree.md` as the authority for the correct mode and `glossary.md Â§Lean â†’ Full step / phase correspondence` for Leanâ†”Full transitions.

changed.title: `AGENTS.md`
changed.body: top-of-file description and `skills/engineering-workflow/README.md`** feature list updated** to name the four execution modes explicitly instead of "Lean / Full modes".

added.title: `docs/glossary.md Â§Lean â†’ Full step / phase correspondence`
added.body: a new mapping table documenting which Full-mode phase each Lean-mode step corresponds to, so that Leanâ†’Full upgrades mid-change re-enter at the correct Full phase mechanically. Prior to this release, mode-upgrade procedure in `mode-decision-tree.md` said "re-enter from the current phase" but did not specify which Full phase a Lean step maps to â€” Lean-2 Minimal Plan merges Full's Phase 2 Plan + Phase 3 Test Plan, Lean-4 Verify covers portions of Full's Phase 5 Review + Phase 6 Sign-off, and Lean has no Phase 8 counterpart, so the mapping was implicit.

fixed.title: Ghost term `ultra-lean`
fixed.body: in `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md Â§Decision table` removed. The term was used in the decision table (`"Tiny single-file fix, no public behavior impact | No skill or ultra-lean"`) but had no definition anywhere in the repo. Replaced with canonical mode names (Zero-ceremony).

fixed.title: Numbering-ambiguity source:
fixed.body: AGENTS.md Â§6's previous "Trivial single-phase tasks (Lean-mode bugfixes, typos) are exempt from the ROADMAP rule" did not reconcile with `SKILL.md Â§Lean workflow`'s Lean-0 â€¦ Lean-5 six-step structure or with `phase-gate-discipline.md:225`'s "single P0 + P7 pair" requirement for single-change initiatives. The three documents now agree: Lean mode has **steps** (six of them) that compress into a **single phase-gate event** at Lean-5 delivery; ROADMAP rows are optional for a Lean-mode single-change initiative; the P0 + P7 minimum applies to Full-mode single-change initiatives only.

fixed.title: Forced-Full trigger list divergence
fixed.body: between `mode-decision-tree.md` and `when-not-to-use-this.md Â§Q3`. Previously `enum` + `rollout` appeared only in the onboarding doc and `cross-team handoff` + `long-lived feature-flag` + `PII` appeared only in the decision tree; the consolidated list now contains all of them (DB migration, public API breaking change, enum consumer-visible, payments, auth/PII, cross-team handoff, long-lived feature-flag, staged rollout) and is maintained in `mode-decision-tree.md` only, with `when-not-to-use-this.md` citing it.

fixed.title: Orphan-mode problem:
fixed.body: `Three-line delivery` was defined in `docs/onboarding/when-not-to-use-this.md` and named in `docs/principles.md Â§8`, but did not appear in `SKILL.md`, `mode-decision-tree.md`, `startup-checklist.md`, or `glossary.md`. An agent walking only the execution layer would never select this mode. Now present and normative across all execution-layer decision aids.

notes.body: Repositories that were writing per-Lean-step gates + per-Lean-step commits + per-Lean-step ROADMAP rows were over-applying Full-mode discipline to Lean-mode work; they may stop. No artifact rewrites are required â€” over-ceremony is not a correctness problem, only an efficiency one.

References to `no-process path`, `ultra-lean`, `No skill`, `three-line handoff`, and `stripped-down version` in project-local documents remain readable; `glossary.md Â§Execution mode` maps each to its canonical name. New local documents should use the canonical names.

Bridge and example files carrying the legacy `Zero ceremony` phrasing in their "Situation | Path" tables are left as-is per `CLAUDE.md Â§3` (factual record preservation for historical content); the glossary's retired-aliases note makes them readable.

fixed.title: `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md:95` â€” broken relative reference to `templates/lean-spec-template.md`.
fixed.body: Rule 5a's Ceremony scaling block pointed at `templates/lean-spec-template.md`; the file actually lives at `skills/engineering-workflow/templates/lean-spec-template.md`. Under either reading convention (relative-to-file â†’ `docs/templates/...`; repo-root â†’ `templates/...`) the prior path did not resolve. Path corrected to `skills/engineering-workflow/templates/lean-spec-template.md`, matching this file's existing repo-root-path convention for all its other cross-reference links (`skills/engineering-workflow/references/resumption-protocol.md`, `skills/engineering-workflow/references/phase-minimums/*`, etc.). Discovered during the summary-vs-per-case audit on 2026-04-22 and recorded as a non-summary-vs-per-case finding in `docs/audits/summary-vs-per-case-registry.md`; now resolved.

fixed.title: `docs/audits/summary-vs-per-case-registry.md` â€” registry updated.
fixed.body: The "Other drift" entry for the `phase-gate-discipline.md:95` broken reference receives a `**Resolved in 1.14.5** (2026-04-23)` note naming the correction and confirming the fix scope is isolated (the other `templates/change-manifest.example-*.yaml` references in `docs/` do resolve correctly against the repo-root `templates/` directory, so no accompanying edits were needed).

fixed.title: `docs/source-of-truth-patterns.md:4` â€” A1: anti-pattern count.
fixed.body: TL;DR "*Five common desync anti-patterns*" corrected to "*Six common desync anti-patterns*" with the six named inline (dual-write, consumer-derived truth, stale cache, doc drift, i18n key drift, pipeline-order as implementation detail). Anti-pattern 6 pairs with sub-pattern 4a Pipeline-Order Contract; the row was added when 4a landed but the TL;DR was never updated. TL;DR also clarifies that half-sync gotchas are a *callout within Pattern 8*, not a separate pattern.

fixed.title: `docs/implementation-disciplines.md:4` â€” A2: axis list mismatch.
fixed.body: TL;DR claimed six axes including two phantom ones ("concurrency + lifecycle", "dependency + environment") that do not exist as axes in the body; the body has five axes including "Documentation sync" that the TL;DR omitted. TL;DR rewritten to name the five actual axes (Contract clarity, Clear layering, Data-access health, Errors and observability, Documentation sync) and to point at `cross-cutting-concerns.md` as the real home of concurrency / cancellation propagation (error-handling sub-section) and dependency / environment concerns (build-time-risk). Reader following the TL;DR is now guided to the correct location for each concern.

fixed.title: `docs/user-surface-disciplines.md:4` â€” A3: axis list mismatch.
fixed.body: TL;DR claimed six axes (flow correctness, contract alignment, feedback + guidance, a11y, responsive + device, analytics + telemetry); body has five with different names (UI flow correctness, Contract alignment, Internationalization and copy, Usability, Operational hooks). Only 2 of 6 TL;DR axes matched the body verbatim. TL;DR rewritten to name the five actual axes â€” a11y and responsive are now correctly folded under "Usability" (as the body organizes them), and "Internationalization and copy" surfaces as its own axis (the TL;DR previously demoted i18n to a sub-bullet).

fixed.title: `docs/adoption-strategy.md:4` â€” A4: stage-duration drift.
fixed.body: TL;DR said "Stage 2 (1-2 months) ... Stage 3 (3+ months)"; body and `docs/diagrams.md:161-162` say 2-4 weeks and 4-8 weeks. TL;DR corrected to match body (2-4 weeks / 4-8 weeks). Adopting teams planning capacity against these durations no longer hit a 2â€“3Ă— error in the TL;DR.

fixed.title: `docs/ci-cd-integration-hooks.md:4` â€” A5: Hook 6 description wrong.
fixed.body: TL;DR said Hook 6 was "Change Manifest schema validation"; body Â§Hook 6 is titled "completion-report check". The actual schema-validation work lives in `docs/automation-contract-algorithm.md` Layer 1, not in this document's hook list. TL;DR corrected: Hook 6 now reads "pre-merge â†’ completion-report check (Full-mode PRs)"; Hook 7 tightened from "observation-window start" to "Phase 8 observation reminder" to match the body. TL;DR also adds a clarifying sentence that schema validation is the automation contract's concern, so the boundary between this doc and the algorithm doc is explicit.

fixed.title: `docs/concurrent-changes.md:4` â€” A6: coordination pattern mismatch.
fixed.body: TL;DR said "Three coordination patterns: sequential, parallel with shared branch, dual-write bridge"; body Â§Coordination strategies has **four** strategies with different names (sequencing, branch isolation + merge-time conflict resolution, joint design + split implementation, merge into one ticket). "Dual-write bridge" does not appear in the body at all â€” the dual-write mechanism is a `breaking-change-framework.md` Path B concern, not a coordination strategy in this doc. TL;DR rewritten to name the four actual strategies.

fixed.title: `docs/system-change-perspective.md:4` + line 9 â€” A7: "six unified questions" is a phantom.
fixed.body: TL;DR claimed this file "lays out the six unified questions" and the body blockquote (line 8â€“9) claimed the list "lives in `docs/surfaces.md`". Neither is true: `surfaces.md` has "six cross-cutting concerns" (different thing â€” line 200) and a "60-second opener" with **five** questions (different content). Both claims removed. TL;DR now describes the **four surface-entry questions** the body actually lays out (inside Â§Don't start by asking "whose layer?") and points at `surfaces.md Â§60-second opener` for the full five-question analysis checklist (which does exist there). Body blockquote correspondingly updated to reference "Â§60-second opener" instead of "six unified analysis questions".

fixed.title: `docs/change-decomposition.md:4` â€” A8: fracture-line under-reporting.
fixed.body: TL;DR named four fracture lines (SoT, surface, consumer pace, risk asymmetry); body Â§Natural fracture lines has six (SoT boundary, surface cadence differs, consumer cohort separation, asymmetric risk level, different reversibility, delivery-sequencing constraint). TL;DR expanded to all six, plus the four merge signals (tightly-coupled atomicity, shared evidence, split would fabricate a scheduling dependency, changes are too small) that were previously unnamed in the TL;DR. Reader using the TL;DR now sees the full decomposition decision surface instead of missing the last two fracture criteria.

changed.title: `docs/audits/summary-vs-per-case-registry.md`
changed.body: updated to mark all eight (a) findings as **Resolved in 1.14.4** with a per-finding resolution note. The registry is kept intact as a dated audit record â€” findings are not deleted, because the registry's own "don't silently re-ship" rule requires resolution to be visible, not erased. The registry's (b) legitimate two-level classifications and (c) boundary-call classifications remain unchanged.

notes.body: Audit produced: `0d8928f docs(audit): summary-vs-per-case consistency registry` (2026-04-22, not a release)

Resolution: **this commit** (`chore(release): 1.14.4`, 2026-04-22) Future audits should follow the same two-commit shape (audit artifact first, resolution release second) so reviewers can separately review (a) the finding list and (b) the chosen resolution direction. Bundling both into a single commit would hide which findings existed before the edits.

fixed.title: `docs/operational-cheat-sheet.md:60` and `docs/onboarding/when-not-to-use-this.md:98` â€” stale "Stage 2" markers removed.
fixed.body: Both rows carried a "*(pending Stage 2)*" / "*(CCKN, once Stage 2 lands)*" qualifier that framed CCKN as a not-yet-shipped feature. The qualifier refers to an internal 1.7.x-era "Stage 2 schema changes" roadmap phase (per the `bebd9c3` commit message "*the agent-protocol-vs-SDD adoption plan*"); the CCKN schema field + canonical doc shipped in the **immediately following** commit `596e5bb` on 2026-04-21, one minute after the markers were written. The markers have therefore been stale from the moment they landed â€” for 14 patch/minor releases through 1.8.0 â†’ 1.14.2. Both rows now point directly at `docs/cross-change-knowledge.md` without the "pending" qualifier. Root cause of the miss: 1.14.0 / 1.14.1 / 1.14.2 all scoped their CCKN reviews to the canonical file + three named consumers (`glossary.md`, `phase1-investigate.md`, `SKILL.md`); the full list of CCKN-mentioning files (10) was not swept until the post-1.14.2 user-requested audit. The two fixes are independent of the 1.14.0â€“1.14.2 normative content â€” they are pre-existing documentation drift that those releases' scope did not cover.

fixed.title: `docs/cross-change-knowledge.md` Â§What to do on no match â€” residual contradiction with the 1.14.1 summary rule
fixed.body: is corrected. 1.14.1 rewrote the Â§When to query summary to say "*write during Phase 1 â€” at initial Investigate per Â§Ceremony scaling, or at re-entry after a Phase 4 Discovery loop*", but left the Â§What to do on no match paragraph saying "*At the end of the change, the Planner evaluates ... â€” that is the write-side rule*." A reader arriving via the "No match" flow therefore still saw end-of-change framed as *the* write point, directly contradicting the corrected summary. The same wording was mirrored in `skills/engineering-workflow/phases/phase1-investigate.md` Â§Startup: query CCKN precedent "No match" bullet. Both are rewritten to make the timing explicit: on no match, a write only happens if Phase 1 itself surfaces reusable knowledge (write during this Phase 1 per Â§Ceremony scaling) or if a Phase 4 Discovery loop later triggers Phase 1 re-entry (write at re-entry per Â§Relation to the Change Manifest Â§3). Otherwise the change produces no CCKN. Root cause: 1.14.1's edit scope was the summary sentence; the downstream prose that carried the same framing was not re-scanned.

fixed.title: `docs/cross-change-knowledge.md` Â§Ceremony scaling â€” query vs write responsibilities per ceremony tier
fixed.body: is now explicit. 1.14.0 / 1.14.1 shipped Â§When to query (query side) and Â§Ceremony scaling (write side) as adjacent but logically disconnected sections â€” neither stated whether Lean mode shares the Phase 1 startup query or whether the Full-mode evaluation in Â§Ceremony scaling also determines *when to write*. 1.14.1's summary sentence "*at initial Investigate per Â§Ceremony scaling*" cross-referenced a section that only said "*evaluates during Phase 1*" â€” a semi-circular pointer. The rewritten Â§Ceremony scaling now: (a) opens with a one-line rule separating query (always on in non-Zero-ceremony tiers) from write (tier-scaled); (b) explicitly states Zero-ceremony skips both query and write; (c) Lean mode runs the query, write is optional, with write-timing tied to Phase 1 (initial or re-entry) rather than end-of-change; (d) Full mode states both evaluation *and* the two possible write timings (available-at-Phase-1 write immediately, otherwise defer to re-entry after Phase 4 Discovery).

fixed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md` Investigate phase block â€” SKILL.md grep-target de-duplication is now complete.
fixed.body: 1.14.1 removed the inline match-handling restatement from SKILL.md but left the grep-target sentence ("*grep the repo's CCKN directory (default: `docs/knowledge/`) for `topics` overlap with anticipated `surfaces_touched`, any `uncontrolled_interfaces`, or libraries / third-party APIs / platforms the change will touch*") duplicated verbatim with the same sentence in `cross-change-knowledge.md:111-115` (canonical) and `phase1-investigate.md:24` (procedural bridge). Three consumer copies of the grep target is exactly the drift pattern 1.14.1 invoked `CLAUDE.md Â§5` to forbid â€” the de-duplication was partial. The paragraph is now a one-sentence discoverability cue plus explicit pointers at the procedural bridge and the canonical rule; the grep target, the three overlap axes, and all match-handling now live only at the canonical + procedural-bridge layer. A future change to the grep target touches two files instead of three.

fixed.title: `CHANGELOG.md` 1.14.0 Â§Why minor â€” forward correction
fixed.body: of the miscited claim "*Lean-mode changes (where CCKN query is optional per Â§Ceremony scaling) all remain valid*." Â§Ceremony scaling (pre-1.14.2) addressed the CCKN **write** side only â€” it never carved out a "Lean-mode query is optional" rule, and the new Â§When to query rule (1.14.0) did not exempt Lean either. The 1.14.0 entry stays intact per `CLAUDE.md Â§3` ("*CHANGELOG is a factual record*"); this bullet documents the correction forward. The actual rule post-1.14.2: the Phase 1 startup query runs in both Lean and Full modes; only the write side is ceremony-scaled. Zero-ceremony continues to skip both.

fixed.title: `docs/cross-change-knowledge.md` Â§When to query a CCKN â€” summary rule
fixed.body: corrected from "*write at Phase 4 Discovery / Phase 1 re-entry; query at Phase 1 Investigate startup*" (the 1.14.0 wording) to "*query at Phase 1 Investigate startup; write during Phase 1 â€” at initial Investigate per Â§Ceremony scaling, or at re-entry after a Phase 4 Discovery loop per Â§Relation to the Change Manifest Â§3. Phase 4 Discovery triggers re-entry; it does not itself write.*" The 1.14.0 summary implied two write locations (Phase 4 Discovery **and** Phase 1 re-entry) and directly contradicted the pre-existing Â§Relation to the Change Manifest Â§3 rule that writing happens "*as part of the Phase 1 Investigate re-entry, **not as a side effect of Phase 4***." A reader following the 1.14.0 summary would conclude Phase 4 is a valid write location, which Â§3 forbids. Root cause: the summary collapsed the trigger source (Phase 4 Discovery) and the write execution point (Phase 1 re-entry) into a single slash-joined phrase without distinguishing them.

fixed.title: `docs/glossary.md` CCKN entry â€” asymmetric-timing paragraph
fixed.body: receives the same correction so the vocabulary-layer summary matches the canonical reference.

fixed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/phases/phase1-investigate.md` Â§Startup: query CCKN precedent
fixed.body: gains the missing **partial match** row (CCKN covers part of the relevant facet â†’ cite for covered + investigate uncovered + extend CCKN if generalizing knowledge emerges). The 1.14.0 phase-file procedural bridge listed only fresh / stale / no match, dropping the "partial match" handling that the canonical Â§When to query a CCKN lists. An agent following the phase file alone would not learn that partial coverage still requires CCKN extension â€” which is load-bearing for write-side discipline. The same section also gains a one-sentence clarification that an absent CCKN directory is treated as no match, not as a failure (removes the risk of a literal `grep` erroring on a non-existent directory and being misread as a tooling failure).

fixed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md` Investigate phase block
fixed.body: reduces the bolded "Startup: query CCKN precedent" paragraph from a full match-handling restatement to a single-sentence pointer at the phase file and the canonical reference. The 1.14.0 version restated fresh / stale / no-match inline, creating **two consumer copies** of the match-handling rule (SKILL.md plus the phase file) â€” exactly the drift pattern `CLAUDE.md Â§5` warns against. The reduced pointer keeps the surface-layer discoverability (an agent reading SKILL.md's Investigate block still sees the rule exists) while leaving the phase file as the single procedural source of truth.

fixed.title: `docs/cross-change-knowledge.md` Â§At Phase 1 Investigate startup
fixed.body: gains the same directory-absent clarification ("an absent directory is not a failure") so the canonical reference and the phase-file bridge agree on the edge case.

added.title: `docs/cross-change-knowledge.md` Â§When to query a CCKN
added.body: new section between Â§Relation to the Change Manifest and Â§What a CCKN is not, closing the long-standing **query-side timing gap** in the 1.8.0 CCKN pattern. Prior state: CCKN's **write** side was prescribed ("*the Planner creates or extends a CCKN as part of the Phase 1 Investigate re-entry*" / "*the Planner evaluates during Phase 1 Investigate whether the change's learning content belongs in a CCKN*") but the **query** side was only passively mentioned ("*Any manifest field that benefits from pre-existing knowledge cites the CCKN by path*") â€” no named procedural step said "query first, before investigation proper begins." Result: agents re-discovered facts that a CCKN would have supplied, and the write-side infrastructure sat unused on the query side. The new Â§When to query defines the **asymmetric lifecycle rule**: **write at Phase 4 Discovery / Phase 1 re-entry; query at Phase 1 Investigate startup**. Section covers: (1) Phase 1 startup grep against `docs/knowledge/` for `topics` overlap with `surfaces_touched` / `uncontrolled_interfaces` / libraries-APIs-platforms-touched; (2) match-handling (fresh = cite + skip re-discovery; stale = cite + inherit refresh obligation per Â§Not a free pass for stale claims + record in `knowledge_notes_touched[]`; partial = cite for covered portion + investigate the rest); (3) no-match fall-through with the existing write-side rule as the end-of-change checkpoint; (4) 5 anti-patterns including the primary one (querying mid-implementation instead of at Phase 1 startup â€” decisions the CCKN should have informed have already been made).

added.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/phases/phase1-investigate.md` â€” new "Startup: query CCKN precedent" section
added.body: terse (~8-line) procedural step positioned before "Investigation dimensions," pointing at the full Â§When to query rule in the canonical reference. Names the three match outcomes (fresh / stale / no match) and the primary anti-pattern (late querying). Keeps the phase-file terse style (~50 lines total including this addition).

changed.title: `docs/glossary.md` â€” CCKN entry
changed.body: gains an asymmetric-lifecycle paragraph: "*write at Phase 4 Discovery / Phase 1 re-entry; query at Phase 1 Investigate startup*." Surfaces the timing distinction at the vocabulary layer so a reader finding "Cross-Change Knowledge Note" in another document knows immediately both when to write and when to query, without having to read the full canonical reference to discover the query side was previously absent.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md` Investigate phase block
changed.body: gains a bolded "Startup: query CCKN precedent" paragraph with the same grep-target / match-handling summary. The skill-layer quick reference now surfaces the rule at the point an agent is deciding Investigate-phase capability priorities, not only when it reads the full phase file.
